doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Hi, um, I’m really worried about my throat. I’ve had this sore throat for like four days now, and it’s getting worse. It hurts a lot when I try to swallow solid foods, and I think I might have a fever, but I haven’t measured it or anything.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the sore throat?
patient_turn: I think it started about four days ago… um, it was just a little scratchy at first, but then it got worse pretty quickly. Now it really hurts, you know?

doctor_turn: Has the pain changed at all since it first started?
patient_turn: Yeah, definitely. It was just a bit annoying at first, but now it’s, like, really painful. I can barely eat anything solid without it hurting a lot. I’m kinda worried it might be something serious.

doctor_turn: Where exactly do you feel the pain in your throat? Does it spread anywhere else?
patient_turn: Um, it’s mostly in the back of my throat, like, right where I swallow. I don’t really feel it anywhere else, though… just there. I don’t have any other symptoms, like a runny nose or cough, so I’m not totally sure what’s going on.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like—sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Oh, you know, I really need to remember to buy some snacks for school.

doctor_turn: What makes the pain better or worse for you?
patient_turn: Um, well, it definitely gets worse when I try to swallow solid foods. I’ve been drinking a lot of water, and that helps a little, but, like, it still hurts. I haven’t really found anything that makes it feel better, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any other symptoms, like feeling short of breath or dizzy?
patient_turn: No, not really. I mean, I haven’t felt short of breath or dizzy at all. It’s just the sore throat and, um, maybe a slight fever, but that’s it. No cough or anything like that, so I’m kinda confused about what’s going on.

doctor_turn: How long have you been experiencing the sore throat?
patient_turn: It’s been about four days now… um, I think I mentioned that earlier. It started off kinda mild, but it’s just gotten worse since then. I really hope it’s nothing serious.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where exactly the pain is in your throat?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, it’s mostly in the back of my throat, like, right where I swallow. It feels really sore there, and it’s hard to eat anything solid. I don’t feel it anywhere else, just that spot.

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like? Is it sharp, dull, or something else?
patient_turn: Uh, it feels... like, um,…

doctor_turn: What does the pain feel like—sharp, dull, or more like pressure?
patient_turn: Um, I’d say it’s more like a sharp pain, especially when I try to swallow. It’s really uncomfortable, and, honestly, it’s hard to ignore. I just wish it would go away.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the pain in your throat?
patient_turn: Um, I think it was maybe four days ago? Or it could’ve been three... I’m not really sure.

doctor_turn: Do you have any past medical conditions or have you had any surgeries?
patient_turn: Um, no, I don’t have any past medical conditions. The only surgery I had was an appendectomy when I was 13. Other than that, I’m pretty healthy, I think.

doctor_turn: Are you currently taking any medications besides multivitamins?
patient_turn: No, I’m not taking any medications besides my multivitamins. I just, um, try to eat healthy and stay active, you know?

doctor_turn: Have you had any other surgeries besides your appendectomy?
patient_turn: No, just the appendectomy when I was 13. I haven’t had any other surgeries or anything like that.

doctor_turn: Are you current on all your vaccinations, except for the HPV and flu vaccines?
patient_turn: Yeah, that’s right. I’m up to date on most of my vaccinations, but I haven’t gotten the HPV vaccine yet, and I didn’t get the flu vaccine for this year. I guess I just kinda forgot about it.

doctor_turn: Who do you live with at home?
patient_turn: I live with my parents and two siblings. It’s, um, pretty busy at home, but I feel safe there.

doctor_turn: Are you currently sexually active or do you plan to be in the near future?
patient_turn: No, I’m not sexually active, and I don’t plan to be before I finish high school. I’m just focusing on school and, um, other stuff right now.

doctor_turn: Do you use alcohol, tobacco, or any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t use any of that stuff. I’ve never tried alcohol or tobacco, and definitely no recreational drugs. I just, um, try to stay healthy, you know?

doctor_turn: How often do you attend school, and have you missed any days recently?
patient_turn: I attend school in a hybrid model, so I go in some days and do online classes on others. I haven’t missed any days recently, but, um, I’m worried I might have to if this throat thing doesn’t get better.

doctor_turn: Have you traveled anywhere recently?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled anywhere recently. I’ve just been at home and going to school, you know? I haven’t been exposed to anyone with COVID-19 or anything like that either.

doctor_turn: Is there any significant medical history in your family, like heart disease or diabetes?
patient_turn: Um, not really, except maybe some possible hypertension in the family. But, like, nothing major that I know of.
